A single BrowserAdapter.ask() method cannot handle all websites. An adapter layer is needed to encapsulate per-site differences.

19.2 Solution: BrowserAdapter + SiteAdapter Dual-Layer Architecture

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         GroupChatBus / Orchestrator          │
│              (Group-chat bus + role orchestration)             │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                   │ ask(prompt, mode=)
                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         BrowserAdapter (Browser Channel)           │
│  · Playwright lifecycle management                    │
│  · storage_state login state reuse                   │
│  · Lazy load + degradation stub mode                        │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                   │ interact(page, prompt, mode)
                   ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         SiteAdapter (Site Adaptation Layer)              │
│  · send_prompt()    — Input box locate + fill       │
│  · select_mode()    — Mode switch               │
│  · extract_stream() — Stream scrape DOM increment       │
│  · wait_for_complete() — Completion detection            │
│  · detect_login_expiry() — Login expiry detection       │
└──────┬──────┬──────┬──────┬──────┬──────────┘
       │      │      │      │      │
       ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼      ▼
   DeepSeek ChatGPT  Qwen  Doubao  KIMI

BrowserAdapter manages the Playwright browser lifecycle and login state, not caring about specific website DOM. SiteAdapter encapsulates per-site DOM interaction differences, exposing a unified interface upward. Adding a new website only requires writing a SiteAdapter subclass + one TOML config file.

19.3 SiteAdapter Abstract Interface

class SiteAdapter(ABC):
    site_name: str

    def __init__(self, profile: SiteProfile) -> None:
        self.profile = profile
        self.safety = SafetyGuard(profile.safety)

    @abstractmethod
    async def send_prompt(self, page, prompt: str) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def select_mode(self, page, mode: str) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def extract_stream(self, page) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def wait_for_complete(self, page) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def detect_login_expiry(self, page) -> bool: ...

    async def interact(self, page, prompt, mode="") -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Complete interaction flow — safety check → login detect → mode select → send → stream → complete"""
        self.safety.check_quota()
        if await self.detect_login_expiry(page):
            raise LoginExpiredError(...)
        if mode:
            await self.safety.human_delay("select_mode")
            await self.select_mode(page, mode)
        await self.safety.human_delay("send_prompt")
        await self.send_prompt(page, prompt)
        self.safety.record_request()
        async for chunk in self.extract_stream(page):
            yield chunk
        await self.wait_for_complete(page)
        self.safety.record_success()

interact() is the main entry called by BrowserAdapter, encapsulating complete safety checks and flow orchestration. Subclasses only need to implement five core methods, without caring about rate limiting, circuit breaking, backoff, and other cross-cutting logic.

19.4 SiteProfile TOML Config System

Each site has one TOML config file storing CSS selectors, mode list, safety parameters, and completion detection strategy. When a site redesigns, change config not code.

# profiles/deepseek.toml
[site]
name = "deepseek"
url = "https://chat.deepseek.com"
login_url = "https://chat.deepseek.com/sign_in"

[selectors]
input_box = "textarea#chat-input"
send_button = "div._77e6e910 button"
response_container = "div.ds-markdown"
stop_button = "div._77e6e910 button[aria-label='Stop']"
login_redirect = "div.login-container"

[[modes]]
name = "deep_thinking"
label = "Deep Thinking"
selector = "div._77e6e910 button:nth-child(1)"

[[modes]]
name = "web_search"
label = "Web Search"
selector = "div._77e6e910 button:nth-child(2)"

[safety]
min_delay = 1.0          # Min delay between operations (seconds)
max_delay = 3.0          # Max delay between operations (seconds)
max_requests_per_session = 50
session_timeout = 3600   # seconds
headed = true            # Headed mode (reduces detection risk)
max_consecutive_errors = 5

[completion]
method = "stop_button_disappear"  # or "response_stable"
timeout = 120                     # seconds
stable_duration = 2.0             # stable duration for response_stable
poll_interval = 0.3               # polling interval

Config files are located in the adapters/sites/profiles/ directory, loaded by site name via load_profile_by_name(). Using TOML format keeps consistency with other project configs (skill.toml, pyproject.toml), no extra dependencies needed.

19.5 SafetyGuard Anti-Ban Protection

SafetyGuard is built into each SiteAdapter, automatically called before and after each browser operation, ensuring behavior patterns approximate real users:

Protection MeasureImplementationDefault
Human-simulation delayhuman_delay() random wait between operations1.0–3.0 seconds
Request rate limitingcheck_quota() checks per-session limit50/session
Session timeoutCircuit breaker when session duration exceeds threshold3600 seconds
Consecutive error circuit breakerStops when consecutive errors reach threshold5 times
Exponential backofferror_backoff() exponential backoff after error2×2^n, max 60s
Headed modeDefault uses visible browser (not headless)headed=true
Login state reuseReuse user's manually logged-in storage_state—

Ban risk assessment: low-medium risk, controllable. High-risk scenarios are "headless + high frequency + multiple accounts same IP". Default config avoids these. Usage advice: use your own account, personal use, initially use headed mode to observe.

19.6 Implementation Hierarchy

SiteAdapter (ABC)                    — Abstract base class + SafetyGuard + interact orchestration
  └─ GenericSiteAdapter               — Pure config-driven generic implementation
       ├─ DeepSeekSite                — Override select_mode (aria-pressed) + detect_login (URL)
       ├─ ChatGPTSite                 — Stub (later override send_prompt for contenteditable)
       ├─ QwenSite                    — Stub (later override select_mode for 6 tabs)
       ├─ DoubaoSite                  — Stub (later override select_mode for quick Q&A toggle)
       └─ KimiSite                    — Stub (later override send_prompt for PPT flow)

GenericSiteAdapter implements all methods' generic logic (driven by profile.selectors), requiring no site-specific code. Site-specific adapters inherit it and override methods needing special handling. DeepSeek is the first fully implemented adapter, serving as the chain-validation baseline. The other four sites use the generic implementation, with site-specific methods progressively overridden in later iterations.

19.7 "Chaining Official Pages" Execution Flow

User enters @deepseek help me analyze this code, then @chatgpt give optimization suggestions in TUI:

1. GroupChatBus receives message, Dispatcher parses @mention
2. Dispatcher assigns tasks to DeepSeek Executor and ChatGPT Executor
3. DeepSeek Executor calls BrowserAdapter.ask(prompt, mode="deep_thinking")
   ├─ BrowserAdapter lazy-loads DeepSeekSite adapter
   ├─ BrowserAdapter lazy-starts Playwright (headed mode)
   ├─ Inject storage_state (user's manually logged-in Cookie)
   ├─ Navigate to chat.deepseek.com
   ├─ DeepSeekSite.interact(page, prompt, "deep_thinking")
   │   ├─ SafetyGuard.check_quota()          ← Rate limit check
   │   ├─ detect_login_expiry(page)           ← Login detection
   │   ├─ SafetyGuard.human_delay("select")  ← 1-3s random delay
   │   ├─ select_mode(page, "deep_thinking")  ← aria-pressed detect + click
   │   ├─ SafetyGuard.human_delay("send")    ← 1-3s random delay
   │   ├─ send_prompt(page, prompt)           ← fill + click
   │   ├─ extract_stream(page)                ← poll DOM increment yield
   │   └─ wait_for_complete(page)             ← stop button disappear
   └─ Stream output yield to GroupChatBus
4. GroupChatBus broadcasts DeepSeek's reply to ChatGPT Executor
5. ChatGPT Executor uses DeepSeek's reply as context, calls BrowserAdapter.ask(...)
6. Both replies displayed as group-chat bubbles in TUI

19.8 Testing Strategy

The SiteAdapter layer's tests do not depend on a real browser, using MockPage to simulate the Playwright Page API:

Test ClassCoverageTest Count
TestSiteProfileData structure immutability, default values, mode list5
TestProfileLoading5 TOML config loading, field validation, error handling8
TestSafetyGuardRate limiting, circuit breaker, delay, backoff, session reset10
TestSiteAdapterBaseinteract complete flow, login expiry, rate limiting, error backoff6
TestDeepSeekSiteSend, mode activation detection, URL login detection, stream scraping10
TestGenericSiteAdapterGeneric send, mode select, stream, completion detection, login detection11
TestSiteStubs4 site stubs' inheritance and basic functionality6
TestSiteAdapterRegistryRegistration, creation, caching, singleton, custom10
TestBrowserAdapterRefactoredSite inference, stub mode degradation, adapter delegation, error handling, resource closing14
TestSiteCapabilityCapability declaration defaults, immutability, TOML loading, custom tags7
TestProfileValidation12 error scenarios: missing fields/URL/selectors/safety params/mode dupes/capability conflicts12
TestProfileDiscoveryMulti-directory search, env vars, user config overrides built-in, custom site loading7
TestRegistryExtensibilityGenericSiteAdapter fallback, unregister, dynamic registration, singleton10

Total 126 unit tests, all passing. Plus existing 175 tests, full 301 tests zero failures. MockPage supports MockElement (text_content / is_visible / click) and StreamingElement (simulates growing response text), fully covering stream scraping and completion detection logic.

19.9 Relationship with Existing Systems

Existing SystemRelationship
Five Channel Types Design (§4)SiteAdapter is the internal implementation layer of the browser channel (③ Web Login), does not affect the other four channel types
Role Orchestration (§5)Executor roles call SiteAdapter indirectly via BrowserAdapter.ask(), agnostic
GroupChat Bus (§6)SiteAdapter's stream output yields directly to the group-chat bus, same format as other channels
Quota-Aware Routing (§8)BrowserAdapter.record_usage() records usage, unified metering with API channels
Configuration Spec (§13)Site TOML config follows the project's unified TOML config spec
Skill System (§18)SiteAdapter is not a Skill, but a channel internal implementation; browser-operation Skills can call BrowserAdapter

The SiteAdapter layer is fully backward compatible: unknown site names degrade to stub mode (outputting prompt text containing "web"), existing tests need no modification. When Playwright is not installed, it also degrades, and the framework runs normally.

20 · Extensible Architecture & One-Click Deployment

20.1 Problem: Long-Term Growth of Site Count

Chapter 19 established the SiteAdapter dual-layer architecture and 5 built-in sites. But the AI industry landscape changes extremely fast — new companies launch new AI products every month, with vastly different DOM structures. If every new site requires framework code changes, maintenance cost grows linearly. A zero-code extension mechanism is needed, so non-core developers can quickly integrate new sites.

20.2 Solution: Four-Tier Extension System

Design a four-tier progressive extension mechanism, reducing the cost of adding a site from "write a class + modify registry" to "place one TOML file":

Extension TierUse CaseOperation CostExample
1. TOML Config FileSites with simple DOM structurePlace .toml in ~/.multimind/sites/New AI company official site, standard textarea + button
2. GenericSiteAdapter FallbackConfig only, no custom codeZero code, Registry auto-fallbackAfter TOML placed, directly create("newsite")
3. Inherit GenericSiteAdapterSite-specific logic neededWrite subclass, override 1-2 methodsDeepSeek's aria-pressed detection
4. Entry Points PluginThird-party package distributionDeclare multimind.sites group in pyproject.tomlpip install multimind-claude auto-registers

The four-tier mechanism is managed uniformly by SiteAdapterRegistry. On first call to get_site_registry(), built-in adapters (tier 3) and Entry Points plugins (tier 4) are auto-registered. On create(), if a site has no custom adapter class but has a TOML config, GenericSiteAdapter is auto-used as fallback (tier 2), dynamically creating a subclass injecting site_name.

20.3 SiteCapability Capability Declaration

Each site's TOML config adds a [capabilities] section, declaring which features the site supports. The orchestrator uses this to decide task allocation — e.g., assigning file analysis tasks to sites supporting file upload, code tasks to sites with code execution capability.

SiteFile UploadMode SwitchMax InputCustom Capabilities
DeepSeekNoYes32K—
ChatGPTYesYes32Kimage_generation, code_interpreter
Tongyi QianwenYesYes30Kimage_generation, video_generation, code_execution
DoubaoYesYes28K—
KIMIYesYes200Kppt_generation, long_context
 
[capabilities]
streaming = true
file_upload = true
multi_turn = true
mode_switching = true
max_input_length = 200000
custom = ["ppt_generation", "long_context"]

SiteCapability is a frozen dataclass containing streaming, file_upload, multi_turn, mode_switching, max_input_length, and custom tuple. validate_profile() validates consistency between capability declaration and config — e.g., error when mode_switching=true but modes is empty.

20.4 Config Validator

validate_profile() validates config legality on load, covering 12 error scenarios:

Required Fields

name, url, input_box, send_button, response_container cannot be empty; url must start with http(s)://

Safety Parameter Sanity

min_delay ≥ 0; max_delay ≥ min_delay; max_requests_per_session, session_timeout, max_consecutive_errors must be > 0

Completion Detection Consistency

method must be stop_button_disappear or response_stable; the former requires non-empty stop_button selector; timeout and poll_interval must be > 0

Mode & Capability Consistency

Mode names cannot duplicate; each mode must have a selector; mode_switching=true requires non-empty modes; max_input_length must be > 0

The validator returns an error list (empty list = pass), does not throw exceptions; caller decides handling. ProfileValidationError exception contains site_name and errors list, for easy problem localization.

20.5 Multi-Directory Config Search

discover_profiles() searches multiple directories by priority; user directory overrides built-in directory (same-name config uses higher priority):

Search order (high → low priority):
1. $MULTIMIND_SITES_DIR (colon-separated multi-dir)
2. ~/.multimind/sites/
3. Built-in profiles/ directory (ships with package)

Users can integrate a new site by placing a config file at ~/.multimind/sites/mysite.toml, no code changes needed. Can also use env var MULTIMIND_SITES_DIR=/path/to/sites to specify a custom directory. This provides a zero-intrusion integration path for "enterprise private AI sites" and "temporary test sites".

20.6 multimind init Config Wizard

The multimind init command provides an interactive config wizard, supporting two modes:

ModeQuestion CountSuitable ForConfig Content
Quick Start (simple)2New usersEnable sites + output directory, rest uses default safety params
Expert Mode (expert)8Advanced usersBrowser headed/headless, safety delay, rate limiting, circuit breaker, custom directory, log level
Non-Interactive (--non-interactive)0CI/CD scriptsAll defaults, no prompts

The generated ~/.multimind/config.toml contains five sections: general/browser/safety/sites/logging. Auto-creates output directory and custom site config directory. --force overwrites existing config, --config path specifies output path.

20.7 install.sh One-Click Install Script

install.sh references best practices from Oh My Zsh and rustup install scripts, implementing a fully automated install flow:

./install.sh                  # Full install (including Playwright browser)
./install.sh --no-browser     # Skip Playwright
./install.sh --venv           # Use virtual environment
./install.sh --dev            # Install dev dependencies
./install.sh --non-interactive # Non-interactive (CI/CD)
./install.sh --uninstall      # Uninstall
FeatureDescription
Python version detectionAuto-detects Python 3.10+, errors and exits if not met
TTY detectionNon-TTY environment auto-switches to non-interactive mode, avoiding CI hang
Env var overrideMULTIMIND_HOME, MULTIMIND_VENV, MULTIMIND_PYTHON
Virtual environment reuse--venv reuses if exists, no duplicate creation
Uninstall flow--uninstall uninstalls pip package + asks to delete config/virtual environment
6-step progressDetect Python → Detect pip → Virtual env → Install deps → Playwright → Verify
Color outputNon-TTY disables colors, avoiding log pollution

20.8 Test Coverage

This batch adds 36 unit tests, covering capability declaration, config validation, multi-directory search, Registry plugin extension:

Test ClassCoverageTest Count
TestSiteCapabilityDefaults, immutability, custom tuple, TOML loading, per-site capability differences7
TestProfileValidation12 error scenarios + ProfileValidationError exception12
TestProfileDiscoveryMulti-directory search, env vars, user overrides built-in, custom site loading7
TestRegistryExtensibilityGenericSiteAdapter fallback, unregister, dynamic registration, singleton10

Full 301 tests pass, ruff lint zero errors. All 5 built-in TOML configs pass validate_profile() validation. GenericSiteAdapter fallback verified by dynamically creating subclass and injecting site_name — sites with only TOML config can directly registry.create("newsite") to get an adapter instance.

20.9 Extension Example: Integrating a New Site

Suppose we want to integrate the "Claude.ai" web version, only three steps needed:

# Step 1: Create TOML config file
cat > ~/.multimind/sites/claude.toml << 'EOF'
[site]
name = "claude"
url = "https://claude.ai"
login_url = "https://claude.ai/login"

[selectors]
input_box = "div[contenteditable='true']"
send_button = "button[aria-label='Send']"
response_container = "div.prose"
stop_button = "button[aria-label='Stop']"

[[modes]]
name = "analysis"
label = "Analysis"
selector = "button[data-mode='analysis']"

[safety]
min_delay = 1.5
max_delay = 3.5
max_requests_per_session = 40

[completion]
method = "stop_button_disappear"
timeout = 150

[capabilities]
streaming = true
file_upload = true
multi_turn = true
mode_switching = true
max_input_length = 100000
custom = ["artifact_analysis"]
EOF

# Step 2: Validate config (optional)
python -c "from multimind.adapters.sites.profile import *; \
  p = load_profile_by_name('claude'); print(validate_profile(p))"

# Step 3: Use directly — no Python code needed
python -c "from multimind.adapters.sites.registry import create_site_adapter; \
  a = create_site_adapter('claude'); print(f'{a.site_name} ready')"

Three steps to integrate a new site: step 1 create config file, step 2 validate (optional), step 3 use directly. Registry auto-discovers TOML config and uses GenericSiteAdapter fallback, zero Python code throughout. If site-specific logic is needed later (e.g., Claude's Artifact render detection), write a ClaudeSite(GenericSiteAdapter) subclass overriding the relevant methods.
